lit: whom he illumined through a voice
sm: whom he gave light to by his voice

syn.mc: Relative clause completing the object of line 700.
syn.sub: The relative clause qualifies τοὺς ἐκ γενετῆς τυφλούς from line 700.
syn.svo: Object: οὗς. Subject: implied Christ. Verb: ἐφωταγώγησεν. Means phrase: διὰ φωνῆς.
syn.rhet: The imperative valuation continues with another healing miracle.

tok: pf mp ptcp acc pl m
lit: Value for me the dead lying there
sm: Set a value for me on the dead lying there

syn.mc: Direct imperative challenge, continued by the relative clause in line 703.
syn.sub: The qualifying relative clause follows in line 703.
syn.svo: Verb: τίμησαί. Dative of interest: μοι. Object: τοὺς κειμένους νεκρούς.
syn.rhet: The taunting valuation challenge now turns to the raising of the dead.

src: οὖς ἐϊς μνημείου ἀνέστησεν ἤδη τεσσάρων ἡμερῶν.
ana: οὖς ἐκ μνημείου ἀνέστησεν ἤδη τεσσάρων ἡμερῶν.
lit: whom he raised from a tomb already four days [dead].
sm: whom he raised from the tomb after four days.
emd: Corrected syntactically impossible ἐϊς to ἐκ.

syn.mc: Relative clause completing the object of line 702.
syn.sub: The relative clause qualifies τοὺς κειμένους νεκρούς.
syn.svo: Object: οὖς. Subject: implied Christ. Verb: ἀνέστησεν. Prepositional phrase: ἐκ μνημείου. Genitive of time/state: τεσσάρων ἡμερῶν.
syn.rhet: The Lazarus motif intensifies the catalogue of unpayable benefactions.

src: ἀτίμητοι αἱ παρ᾽ αὐτοῦ σοι δωρεαί"
ana: ἀτίμητοι αἱ παρ᾽ αὐτοῦ σοι δωρεαί
lit: Priceless are the gifts from him to you
sm: The gifts from him to you are beyond price

syn.mc: Nominal main clause.
syn.svo: Subject: αἱ ... δωρεαί. Predicate adjective: ἀτίμητοι. Prepositional phrase: παρ᾽ αὐτοῦ. Dative of advantage: σοι.
syn.rhet: The catalogue pauses to state directly the immeasurable worth of the gifts.

src: σὺ δὲ ἀτίμως ἀνταπέδωκας εἰς αὐτὸν τὰς χάριτας,
lit: but you dishonorably repaid the favors toward him,
sm: but you repaid his favors to him dishonorably,

syn.mc: Main adversative clause.
syn.svo: Subject: σὺ. Verb: ἀνταπέδωκας. Object: τὰς χάριτας. Direction phrase: εἰς αὐτόν. Adverb: ἀτίμως.
syn.rhet: The rhetoric turns from gift to grotesque counter-gift.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: having repaid ingratitudes to him,
sm: repaying him with ingratitude,

syn.mc: Participial phrase dependent on line 706.
syn.svo: Participle: ἀνταποδοὺς, modifying the subject σὺ. Object: ἀχαριστίας. Dative: αὐτῷ.
syn.ptc: ἀνταποδοὺς elaborates the manner of ἀνταπέδωκας.
syn.rhet: The participle sharpens the moral inversion with a compact accusatory formula.

lit: evil things instead of good things
sm: evil in return for good,

syn.mc: Elliptical accusative phrase dependent on line 707.
syn.svo: No finite verb. The phrase states the content of the repayment: κακὰ ἀντὶ καλῶν.
syn.rhet: The antithetical exchange is cast in terse, balanced terms.

lit: and affliction instead of joy
sm: and affliction in place of joy,
emd: Corrected obvious OCR corruption θλῦψιν to θλῖψιν.

syn.mc: Second elliptical exchange phrase dependent on line 707.
syn.svo: No finite verb. Coordinated accusative phrase: θλῖψιν ἀντὶ χαρᾶς.
syn.rhet: The antithetical pair continues the rhythmic counter-gift pattern.

lit: and death instead of life
sm: and death in place of life,

syn.mc: Third elliptical exchange phrase dependent on line 707.
syn.svo: No finite verb. Coordinated accusative phrase: θάνατον ἀντὶ ζωῆς.
syn.rhet: The exchange list reaches its climax in life-for-death reversal.

src: ὑπὲρ οὗ καὶ ἀποθανεῖν σε ἔδει.
lit: for whom it was necessary even for you to die.
sm: for him, you ought even to have died.

syn.mc: Impersonal main clause with infinitive subject.
syn.sub: The relative phrase ὑπὲρ οὗ refers back to the benefactor just described.
syn.svo: Impersonal verb: ἔδει. Infinitive subject: ἀποθανεῖν σε. Relative prepositional phrase: ὑπὲρ οὗ.
syn.rhet: The accusation culminates by reversing the proper direction of death.

src: Εἶτα ἐὰν μὲν ἔθνους ἁρπαγῇ βασιλεὺς ὑπὸ ἐχθρῶν,
lit: Then if a king of a nation be seized by enemies,
sm: Then if a king of some nation is seized by enemies,

syn.mc: Conditional protasis introducing an analogy.
syn.sub: ἐὰν introduces the condition; the apodosis unfolds in lines 713-719.
syn.svo: Subject: βασιλεύς. Verb: ἁρπαγῇ. Genitive modifier: ἔθνους. Agent phrase: ὑπὸ ἐχθρῶν.
syn.rhet: A political analogy is introduced to expose Israel's contrary behavior.

tok: is set in motion; is organized
lit: for him war is set in motion,
sm: for his sake war is set in motion,

syn.mc: First clause of the apodosis.
syn.svo: Subject: πόλεμος. Verb: συνίσταται. Prepositional phrase: δι᾽ αὐτόν.
syn.rhet: The response to a captured king is described in rapid parataxis.

lit: for him a wall is broken,
sm: for his sake a wall is breached,

syn.mc: Second clause of the apodosis.
syn.svo: Subject: τεῖχος. Verb: ῥήγνυται. Prepositional phrase: δι᾿ αὐτόν.
syn.rhet: Military effort escalates from mobilization to assault.

lit: for him a city is seized,
sm: for his sake a city is stormed,

syn.mc: Third clause of the apodosis.
syn.svo: Subject: πόλις. Verb: ἀναρπάζεται. Prepositional phrase: δι᾿ αὐτόν.
syn.rhet: The parallelism continues with another civic image of rescue effort.

lit: for him ransom is sent,
sm: for his sake ransom is sent,

syn.mc: Fourth clause of the apodosis.
syn.svo: Subject: λύτρα. Verb: πέμπεται. Prepositional phrase: δι᾿ αὐτόν.
syn.rhet: From force the analogy turns to negotiated recovery.

lit: for him envoys are sent
sm: for his sake envoys are sent

syn.mc: Fifth clause of the apodosis.
syn.sub: The purpose alternatives follow in lines 718-719.
syn.svo: Subject: πρέσβεις. Verb: ἀποστέλλονται. Prepositional phrase: δι᾽ αὐτόν.
syn.rhet: The sequence narrows to diplomatic recovery, preparing the purpose clauses.

tok: pres act ptcp nom sg m
lit: or in order that, alive, he may be recovered
sm: either that he may be recovered alive

syn.mc: First purpose alternative dependent on line 717.
syn.sub: ἵνα introduces a purpose clause coordinated with line 719.
syn.svo: Subject: implied king. Verb: ἀναλημφθῇ. Predicative participle: ζῶν.
syn.ptc: ζῶν is predicative, stating the condition in which the king is recovered.
syn.rhet: The first alternative imagines successful rescue.

lit: or in order that, dead, he may be buried.
sm: or that, if dead, he may be buried.

syn.mc: Second purpose alternative dependent on line 717.
syn.sub: ἵνα introduces a purpose clause coordinated with line 718.
syn.svo: Subject: implied king. Verb: ταφῇ. Predicative adjective: νεκρός.
syn.rhet: The second alternative shows that even a corpse receives honor.

src: σὺ δὲ τὴν ἐναντίαν κατὰ τοῦ κυρίου σου ἤνεγκας ψῆφον"
ana: σὺ δὲ τὴν ἐναντίαν κατὰ τοῦ κυρίου σου ἤνεγκας ψῆφον
lit: but you cast the opposite verdict against your Lord
sm: but you cast the contrary verdict against your Lord

syn.mc: Main adversative clause applying the analogy.
syn.sub: The relative elaboration follows in lines 721-724.
syn.svo: Subject: σὺ. Verb: ἤνεγκας. Object: τὴν ἐναντίαν ψῆφον. Prepositional phrase: κατὰ τοῦ κυρίου σου.
syn.rhet: The contrastive σὺ δέ marks Israel's conduct as the inverse of ordinary loyalty.

lit: for whom the nations were worshiping
sm: for the one whom the nations worshiped

syn.mc: Relative clause modifying κυρίου in line 720.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause beyond the relative construction.
syn.svo: Object: ὃν. Subject: τὰ ἔθνη. Verb: προσεκύνουν.
syn.rhet: The indictment broadens by contrasting Israel with the nations.

lit: and the uncircumcised were marveling
sm: and the uncircumcised marveled

syn.mc: Coordinate clause within the relative sequence.
syn.svo: Subject: ἀκρόβυστοι. Verb: ἐθαύμαζον. Object remains the relative antecedent from line 721.
syn.rhet: A second Gentile witness reinforces the shame of Israel's action.

lit: and foreigners were glorifying,
sm: and foreigners were glorifying him,
emd: Removed stray OCR quotation mark before the comma.

syn.mc: Third coordinate clause within the relative sequence.
syn.svo: Subject: ἀλλόφυλοι. Verb: ἐδόξαζον. Object remains understood from the relative antecedent.
syn.rhet: The triple Gentile response forms a rising parallelism of reverence.

src: ἐφ᾽ ᾧ καὶ Πιλᾶτος ἐνίψατο τὰς χεῖρας,
lit: over whom even Pilate washed his hands,
sm: over whom even Pilate washed his hands,

syn.mc: Relative prepositional clause continuing the elaboration of line 720.
syn.sub: The relative phrase ἐφ᾽ ᾧ refers back to the Lord.
syn.svo: Subject: Πιλᾶτος. Verb: ἐνίψατο. Object: τὰς χεῖρας. Relative prepositional phrase: ἐφ᾽ ᾧ.
syn.rhet: Even Pilate is presented as recoiling where Israel did not.

src: σὺ τοῦτον ἀπέκτεινας ἐν τῇ μεγάλῃ ἑορτῇ.
lit: you killed this one in the great feast.
sm: you killed this one at the great feast.

syn.mc: Main clause concluding the accusation.
syn.svo: Subject: σὺ. Verb: ἀπέκτεινας. Object: τοῦτον. Locative phrase: ἐν τῇ μεγάλῃ ἑορτῇ.
syn.rhet: The charge lands bluntly after the long relative buildup.

src: Tοιγαροῦν πικρά σοι ἡ τῶν ἀζύμων ἑορτὴ καθώς σοι γέγραπται"
ana: Τοιγαροῦν πικρά σοι ἡ τῶν ἀζύμων ἑορτὴ καθώς σοι γέγραπται·
lit: Therefore bitter to you is the feast of unleavened bread, just as it has been written for you:
sm: Therefore the feast of unleavened bread is bitter to you, as it stands written for you:
emd: Normalized mixed Latin/Greek initial T to Greek Τ, removed trailing OCR quotation mark, and regularized citation punctuation.

syn.mc: Nominal main clause introducing a scriptural citation.
syn.svo: Subject: ἡ τῶν ἀζύμων ἑορτή. Predicate adjective: πικρά. Dative: σοι. Citation verb: γέγραπται.
syn.rhet: The accusation turns into a curse-like reversal of Passover imagery.

lit: You will eat unleavened things with bitters.
sm: You shall eat unleavened bread with bitter herbs.

syn.mc: Quoted scriptural clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied ὑμεῖς. Verb: Ἔδεσθε. Object: ἄζυμα. Prepositional phrase: μετὰ πικρίδων.
syn.rhet: The biblical prescription becomes the lens for the bitter recompense.

lit: bitter to you are the nails which you sharpened,
sm: bitter to you are the nails that you sharpened,
emd: Corrected likely breathing error ὥξυνας to ὤξυνας.

syn.mc: Nominal curse-like clause with a relative modifier.
syn.sub: The relative clause οὖς ὤξυνας qualifies ἧλοι.
syn.svo: Subject: ἧλοι. Predicate adjective: πικροί. Dative: σοι. Relative clause verb: ὤξυνας.
syn.rhet: The nails become a bitter recompense to their maker.

lit: bitter to you is the tongue which you stirred up,
sm: bitter to you is the tongue that you stirred up,

syn.mc: Nominal curse-like clause parallel to line 729.
syn.sub: The relative clause ἣν παρώξυνας qualifies γλῶσσα.
syn.svo: Subject: γλῶσσα. Predicate adjective: πικρά. Dative: σοι. Relative clause verb: παρώξυνας.
syn.rhet: The parallel bitter clauses create a judicial litany of retribution.
